summary:
Angina occurs when there’s an imbalance between myocardial oxygen supply and demand. Stable angina specifically arises from such mismatches during increased activity or stress leading to transient ischemia.

The summary provided above contains only two sentences focused solely on clinically significant facts related to the condition being discussed—namely, defining angina through its fundamental mechanism involving oxygen supply-demand imbalance—and clearly identifies stable angina within context without unnecessary elaboration beyond essential pathophysiology. It avoids tangential details while maintaining precision regarding both cause and manifestation based upon established medical knowledge.
